item.title: Iran War Tightens the Squeeze on Americans as Inflation Climbs
item.description: The worst US inflation in years is once again eating away at Americans’ paychecks, as rising gasoline and grocery prices threaten to undermine household spending.

item.title: Petrobras Completes First Phase of $470 Million Data Center Plan
item.description: Elea Data Centers has completed the first phase of a 2.3 billion reais ($470 million) project for Brazilian state-controlled oil producer Petrobras, as cloud computing expands in Latin America’s largest economy.

item.title: US Power Prices Climb 61% Faster Than Inflation as Demand Surges
item.description: Consumer prices climbed last month by the most in three years, but prices for electricity surged even more, highlighting an intensifying battle between utilities, consumers and power grids.

item.title: US LNG Pioneer Charif Souki Vows He Will Never Go Public Again
item.description: Natural gas entrepreneur Charif Souki’s latest venture will remain closely held after his previous two companies pursued public offerings.

item.title: India Books Phosphate Fertilizer at 40% Above Pre-War Prices
item.description: India, the world’s top buyer of diammonium phosphate, has contracted the crop nutrient at prices almost 40% higher than pre-war levels as the Middle East conflict disrupts supplies and pushes global benchmarks higher.

item.title: France’s Economy Is Succumbing to Iran War Shock, Survey Shows
item.description: France’s economy is showing signs of faltering as the fallout from the Middle East conflict hits growth and ratchets up inflation pressure, according to the central bank’s monthly survey of companies.

item.title: Iran’s Kharg Island Oil Shipments Show First Prolonged Halt Since Start of War
item.description: Oil shipments from Iran’s main export terminal appear to have come to a standstill over the past several days, according to satellite images, the first sign of a prolonged halt since the start of the war.

item.title: Senegal’s Faye, IMF Agree to Press Ahead With Debt Talks
item.description: Senegal and the International Monetary Fund agreed to continue talks to address the fallout from billions of dollars in previously undisclosed debt and map a way forward.

item.title: Providence Equity Considers Acquisition of Gamma Communications
item.description: Providence Equity Partners is among suitors exploring an acquisition of Gamma Communications Plc, the London-listed telecommunication services company, people with knowledge of the matter said.

item.title: ECB Rate Hikes Increasingly Likely, Nagel Tells Handelsblatt
item.description: The probability that the European Central Bank will need to raise borrowing costs due to the Iran war is rising, according to Bundesbank President Joachim Nagel.

item.title: Junk-Rated Firms Rush to Reprice Debt as Demand for Loans Surges
item.description: Junk-rated firms are rushing to reprice and refinance existing US-dollar debt, seizing on renewed investor appetite for riskier loans to slash borrowing costs.

item.title: Altman Testifies About ‘Hair-Raising’ AI Safety Chat With Musk
item.description: OpenAI’s Sam Altman testified that he was “extremely uncomfortable” with Elon Musk’s insistence that he have complete control over a proposed for-profit subsidiary of OpenAI back in 2017.

item.title: Brazil Meat Left Out of Europe Suppliers List in Risk to Trade
item.description: Brazil is absent from a list of countries authorized to supply animal products to the European Union, presenting a trade risk to the world’s largest beef exporter.

item.title: Russia Sees 2026 Oil Output Flat as Kyiv Steps Up Drone Strikes
item.description: Russia expects its oil production to remain flat in 2026 and grow modestly in the coming two years as its energy infrastructure comes under intense drone attacks from Ukraine.

item.title: Google Debuts Android ‘Googlebook’ Laptop Platform, Reentering Hot Market
item.description: Alphabet Inc.’s Google introduced a new high-end laptop segment called "Googlebook" that will run Android and prominently showcase Gemini artificial intelligence. Hardware partners Dell Technologies Inc., Lenovo Group Ltd. and HP Inc. will debut models built on the platform in the coming months.

item.title: Anthropic Expands Push Into Legal Industry With New AI Tools
item.description: Anthropic PBC is taking steps to help legal professionals carry out more tasks using its Claude chatbot, months after the startup helped spark a steep selloff in software stocks by quietly introducing a narrower set of features.Anthropic on Tuesday announced a dozen new tools, or plugins, intended for attorneys, law students and others in the legal sector. One feature, called “commercial counsel,” is meant to take on work like reviewing vendor agreements; another tool is intended to help with st

item.title: Google Unveils New Android AI Features Ahead of Apple’s Siri Revamp
item.description: Alphabet Inc.’s Google announced a slew of upcoming features for its Android 17 operating system, showcasing its progress in artificial intelligence weeks before Apple Inc. is set to announce major upgrades to its own iOS platform and AI offerings.

item.title: An 82% Earnings Drop at US LBM Shows Pain in Building Materials
item.description: Building materials and products distributor US LBM Holdings reported sharply weaker first-quarter earnings as operating expenses ramped up and demand continued to soften in one of the starkest examples yet of the pain engulfing the industry.

item.title: Anthropic Warns Investors to Avoid Certain Secondary Market Sellers
item.description: Anthropic PBC identified a number of secondary marketplaces as unauthorized sellers of the company’s shares, telling investors that buying the stock won’t work.

item.title: New Jersey ICE Warehouse Paused as US to Weigh Green Impact
item.description: The federal government agreed to review the environmental impact of a proposed immigrant detention facility in New Jersey and temporarily pause a lawsuit seeking to block the conversion of an industrial warehouse.

item.title: Google in Talks to Use SpaceX to Launch Space Data Centers, Report Says
item.description: Alphabet Inc.’s Google is in talks with Elon Musk’s SpaceX to launch the search company’s orbital data centers, the Wall Street Journal reported on Tuesday.

item.title: US to Unveil New Data Spotlighting Hormuz and Global Reserves
item.description: The US government’s energy statistics agency will start releasing new data on the world’s strategic reserves and flows of petroleum and liquefied natural gas through shipping choke points.

item.title: Chile’s Populist Renegade Is Getting His Way on Kast’s Big Bill
item.description: Chile’s Franco Parisi, the third-place finisher in last year’s presidential election, is proving to be a formidable political operative, strong-arming the government to accept his populist initiatives in exchange for supporting its economic agenda.

item.title: Kazakh CPC Oil Exports to Drop as Europe Faces Tight Supplies
item.description: Kazakhstan will cut crude exports from a key Russian Black Sea port next month at a time when European refineries are already grappling with an unprecedented supply disruption from the Middle East.

item.title: Biggest US Grid May Be Too Large to Function, Regulator Says
item.description: PJM Interconnection LLC, the biggest power grid in the US, may have become too big to adequately function and needs urgent reform, Federal Energy Regulatory Commission Chair Laura Swett said at the grid operator’s annual meeting.

item.title: Venture Global Shares Surge on LNG Deals, Project Expansions
item.description: Venture Global Inc. shares shot up on Tuesday after the liquefied natural gas exporter announced two new supply deals and detailed expansion plans for export projects in Louisiana.

item.title: Alphabet Offers Eight-Tranche Deal in Its Debut Yen Bond Sale
item.description: Alphabet Inc. has begun marketing its first-ever bond sale in yen, further broadening its funding channels as the Google parent sharply increases capital spending to finance artificial intelligence efforts.

item.title: US Soy Growers Want Commitments From China as Season Slips Away
item.description: As US President Donald Trump travels to Beijing for a summit this week with his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping, American soybean farmers are in a now-familiar spot: Fields are getting planted when demand from their biggest customer is uncertain.

item.title: Ex-Eaton Vance CEO Takes Majority Stake in Mutual Fund Rescuer
item.description: Thomas Faust, who engineered the sale of Eaton Vance to Morgan Stanley, is beginning his next act: helping out mutual fund managers who are facing redemption requests.

item.title: EQT Taps Qatari Funding as Bids for €8 Billion VW Unit Near
item.description: Swedish buyout firm EQT AB is tapping funding from the Qatari sovereign fund as it prepares to make a binding bid for Volkswagen AG’s large marine engine unit by a deadline next month, people with knowledge of the matter said.
